title: Memory Weights (Reinforcement and Decay)
description:
Implement the memory concept: clusters that appear repeatedly across runs get reinforced (higher weight), while clusters that stop appearing decay. This is the mechanism that distinguishes persistent trends from transient noise.

Each cluster carries a `memory_weight` (float, 0.0 to 1.0) and a `memory_tier` (hot/warm/cold). On each run:
- Clusters that received new documents are **reinforced** (weight moves toward 1.0)
- Clusters with no new documents **decay** (weight moves toward 0.0)
- Tiers are derived from weight thresholds (hot >= 0.7, warm >= 0.3, cold < 0.3)
- Clusters below a prune threshold can be removed entirely

## Definition of Done

- Pure-logic weight computation functions (reinforce, decay, tier assignment, prune check)
- Integration with OpenSearch cluster records: bulk update weights after each run
- Pruned clusters removed from index, their items marked as unassigned
- All scenarios in child stories pass

## Depends On

Epic plx-aaeca3 (Global Clustering Engine) — for cluster records

## Key Files

- Create: `plexus/analysis/memory_weights.py`
- Create: `plexus/analysis/memory_weights_test.py`
- Modify: `plexus/analysis/opensearch_client.py` (add bulk update method)
